summaries.144224: Comments confirm the issue is not limited to Samsung Pass: Kaspersky, KeePassDX, ProtonPass, and the C2 Password browser extension all trigger the same unfocus behavior on Android. One commenter extends the scope to Flutter Web, where password manager autofill via browser extension causes fields to lose focus on Windows (Chrome), macOS (Chrome/Firefox), and Android (Chrome). The issue is not reproduced in native Android apps, indicating it is Flutter-specific. Status: open, no fix or PR linked.
summaries.145839: Comments confirm the scrollbar-not-reaching-bottom bug is limited to mobile (iOS and Android); macOS is unaffected. The root cause is traced to `ScrollbarPainter._totalTrackMainAxisOffsets` using `padding.vertical` instead of 0. Two workarounds are shared: wrapping with `SafeArea` (partial fix) or wrapping the `Scrollbar`/`ListView` with `MediaQuery.removePadding(removeBottom: true)`. Status: open, no PR, workaround available.
summaries.134717: Comments confirm the VoiceOver announcement gap is iOS-only; Android does not have the issue. Reproduced on both stable 3.13.4 and master. The issue was CC'd to @hangyujin for iOS accessibility input, and is labeled for both design and iOS teams. Status: stalled, no fix or PR, assigned to team-design.
summaries.163534: Comments confirm reproducibility on both stable 3.29.0 and master 3.30 on web (Chrome). A reviewer notes a related issue: drag-to-select text inside a TextField inside HtmlElementView also misbehaves and should be investigated together. The root cause (space/Enter keystrokes leaking to the parent Flutter ScrollView instead of being consumed by the HTML input element) is not yet diagnosed. Status: open, no fix or PR.
summaries.169447: Comments confirm the bug on Windows 10 and 11 (stable 3.32.0 and master 3.33.0): the first Enter key press after re-focusing the app refocuses the window rather than triggering the action, requiring a second press. Reproduced using url_launcher to open an external browser. A reviewer suggests this may be related to recent focus changes. A PR #178523 was submitted to fix the issue. Status: has PR #178523.
summaries.113944: Comments identify the root cause as an interaction between Japanese IME (Windows prompt window) and `inputFormatters` using a regex decimal pattern: when the IME commits a candidate, the text is duplicated. A commenter suggests the fix is to include `composing: newValue.composing` when building the new `TextEditingValue` in the input formatter. Reproduced on both stable 3.3.5 and master. Status: open, no PR, fix direction identified.
summaries.27529: Comments confirm the bug is reproducible on stable 3.3.8 and master 3.6. A commenter notes the behavior varies: standard web browser multiline text fields do not snap the cursor, while Android's messaging app does, suggesting it may not be the universal default. The issue is on Android and possibly other platforms. Status: open, stalled, no fix or PR.
summaries.14331: Comments provide a detailed root cause: Flutter's async/sync gap means `TYPE_VIEW_TEXT_CHANGED` is sent after TalkBack's `afterCut()` timestamp window, so TalkBack does not associate the event with the cut operation. Sending a fake anticipatory event or manually setting the event timestamp does not work. Behavior has partially improved over time (paste now announces "pasted texts" then reads all content), but the correct announcement for cut/paste actions is still missing on Android. Status: stalled, no PR, engine-level issue.
summaries.124384: Comments confirm the selection cursor (text cursor) still appears over `WidgetSpan` children even when wrapped in `SelectionContainer.disabled`, reproduced on master as of stable 3.7 and master 3.9. The root cause is that the outer `Text` widget wraps the entire content (including `WidgetSpan`) with a `MouseRegion` that sets the text cursor. Proposed fix: have `SelectionContainer.disabled` wrap its child with a `MouseRegion` to override the cursor; a workaround is to wrap the `WidgetSpan` child with a `MouseRegion(cursor: SystemMouseCursors.basic)`. Status: open, no PR, workaround available.
summaries.95556: Comments confirm the issue is still present on Windows with NVDA screen reader, exactly three years after the original report. Arrow key navigation through text field content produces no speech output. Reproduced on stable 2.8.1 and master. The issue may also affect web (other UI elements not read there). Status: stalled, no PR, team-windows assigned.
summaries.140790: Comments clarify the Neo2 keyboard layout issue: the modifier keys M4/Mod4 (like AltGr) on a physical ISO keyboard should produce arrow/control key events when combined with WASD/ESDF, but Flutter on Linux desktop does not recognize them, while Flutter on web and other native Linux apps work correctly. Reproduction requires a physical ISO keyboard with an AltGr key; the triager's ANSI keyboard cannot reproduce it. Status: stalled, no PR, team-linux assigned.
summaries.63226: Comments identify the root cause as an invalid (negative) selection state after `focusNode.requestFocus()` sets focus without a valid cursor position, causing a `RangeError` on paste. The issue was confirmed on Windows stable 2.0.1 and master 1.27.0. A commenter suspects the same bug exists on macOS and Linux since the shortcut handler logic is shared. The web build skips the handler and is unaffected. Status: stalled, no PR.
summaries.154515: Comments confirm the Autocomplete overlay remains open and follows the field out of view on both web and desktop (stable 3.24.1, master 3.25). This is noted as similar to issue #150048. The issue was previously fixed by #154675 but that PR was reverted in #161277, leaving the bug unresolved. An automated reminder notes the issue is assigned to @Renzo-Olivares with no recent updates. Status: landed-but-reopened (fix reverted), no active PR.
summaries.182059: Comments confirm the issue is iOS/Android-safe but affects macOS, Windows, and Linux: after Navigator.pop, the `FocusScopeNode` of the modal scope receives primary focus instead of delegating it back to the `TextField`. Testing with `EditableText` alone reproduces the issue, confirming it is not a `TextField`-specific bug. Two workarounds are provided in the issue body itself. Status: open, no PR, affects macOS/Windows/Linux only.
summaries.110035: Comments clarify that this is by-design: `EditableText` explicitly enables scrollbars for multi-line text fields regardless of the app-level `ScrollBehavior`. The team acknowledges the documentation is misleading. A workaround is provided: wrap the `TextField` with a `ScrollConfiguration` using a custom `ScrollBehavior` that overrides `buildScrollbar` to return the child directly. A request was made to add a `scrollBehavior` parameter to `TextField`/`TextFormField`. Status: stalled, doc update proposed, workaround available.
summaries.108016: Comments confirm the root cause: after moving the cursor within the composing region via the floating cursor (long-press space), the iOS platform text input plugin continues treating the cursor as being at the end of the composing region. A cast error (`int` to `double`) also appears during floating cursor updates. The issue affects both Chinese pinyin and Japanese kana keyboards, not English. Moving the cursor outside the composing region commits the text (different from native behavior). A related issue #122490 was filed for the native snap-back behavior. A UIKit app reproducing the bug was created, and a radar was filed with Apple since the fix may require a secret UITextInput method call. Status: stalled, waiting on Apple/engine.
summaries.173488: Comments provide a detailed engine-level analysis: the spell-check highlight for `omw` is triggered via a faster `UIKeyboardTaskQueue` path (not deferred like autocorrect), so `firstRectForRange:` is called before the Flutter framework has produced a new frame with the correct text layout for `omw`. The root cause is a timing issue where the iOS system requests the text rectangle before Flutter's rendering reflects the latest text state. Status: open, no fix or PR.
summaries.44681: Comments are predominantly user requests and workarounds with no official fix. The only actionable content: on Android, a platform channel workaround using `InputMethodManager.hideSoftInputFromWindow` is described in detail. The `triaged-desktop` label cleanup bot commented. No Flutter team member has provided a concrete fix plan or timeline. Status: stalled, workaround available (Android platform channel), no PR.
summaries.93778: Comments confirm that when Ctrl is held and a mouse click occurs on macOS in Chrome, the browser shows the right-click context menu, which causes the browser to swallow the Ctrl key-up event and Flutter never receives it. Confirmed on stable 2.5.3 and master 2.6. The issue was CC'd to @dkwingsmt. Status: stalled, no PR.
summaries.149979: Comments confirm the IME conversion highlight/caret misalignment occurs in Chrome on desktop (macOS) when using non-English (e.g., Japanese) keyboards in Flutter web; it does not affect English input. Reproduced on stable 3.22.2 and master 3.23. Assigned to both web and text-input teams but the multi-team label assignment is flagged as improper. Status: stalled, no fix or PR.
summaries.91798: Comments confirm the issue is specific to iBus/fcitx being enabled on Linux Desktop: when either input method is active, normal keyboard input (English letters, space) does not reach the Flutter text field, though delete works and IME-composed characters do appear. Disabling iBus fixes the issue. A `KeyUpEvent` assertion error appears in the logs when iBus is enabled. The team linked this to a similar issue #87391. Status: stalled, no fix or PR, team-text-input assigned.
summaries.174773: Comments confirm password manager autofill on web is a long-standing and broad issue, distinct from more specific prior reports. Bitwarden's keyboard shortcut autofill regressed between 3.32.8 and 3.35.2. Proton Pass does not work even after downgrading. The issue is still reproducible as of 3.41.4 and 3.41.6. The reporter links related issues #127694, #66356, and #156235. Some users report partial autofill issues specifically on iOS/iPadOS/Android Chrome browsers. Status: open, no fix or PR, team-web assigned.
summaries.152879: Comments confirm the form is immediately autofilled (without user selecting credentials) on first click, specifically in Chrome; Safari works correctly. The console error was fixed by an engine PR #53830, but the autofill behavior issue persists. Reproduced on master 3.24.0. The issue affects both debug and release modes with slightly different behaviors. Multiple users still report the issue as of stable 3.35.6 and 3.35.7. Workaround: double-click or delete and retype to trigger the password manager dropdown. Status: stalled, no fix or PR.
summaries.112762: Comments report the iOS keyboard slowness/non-responsiveness is specific to the simulator on iOS 16 and is not reproduced on native non-Flutter apps. The issue does not reproduce consistently across testers (some cannot reproduce it at all on the same hardware/simulator combination). Reports also indicate the issue on physical iOS 15.x devices. The team requested an Instruments time profile recording for further investigation, which was never provided. Status: stalled, cannot consistently reproduce, no fix or PR.
summaries.148936: Comments confirm the right Control key on an external keyboard is not recognized by `HardwareKeyboard` / `onKeyEvent` on macOS, while the old `onKey`/`RawKeyboard` API correctly fires for both left and right Control keys. Reproduced on stable 3.22.0 and master 3.22.0-45. The issue was forwarded to @dkwingsmt. Status: stalled, no fix or PR, team-macos assigned.
summaries.139301: Comments confirm the trailing-space rendering bug occurs not only on Windows but also macOS, web, and Android. The space character is actually present in the text (pressing backspace twice confirms this), but is not rendered because `TextAlign.end` trims trailing whitespace in the display. A workaround using ` ` (non-breaking space) instead of a regular space is provided. A commenter suggests this is a duplicate of issue #40648. Status: open, possibly duplicate of #40648, workaround available.
summaries.40682: Comments from the engine team confirm spellcheck requires: (1) scroll position sync between the hidden `<input>` element and Flutter's text field, (2) keeping the `<input>` alive and positioned after blur, and (3) keeping styling in sync. A JS-based workaround using `setAttribute('spellcheck', 'true')` on focus is provided and documented, but the squiggly underlines appear offset from the actual text position. The workaround does not work with semantics enabled. A prior engine PR #12192 enabled spellcheck for most browsers but not Safari Mobile. Status: stalled, no framework/engine fix, workaround available with known offset defect.
summaries.100342: Comments confirm that when the emoji keyboard's search field is used and then the Flutter TextField is tapped, focus does not switch — the emoji search field retains focus and the Flutter TextField cannot receive input or delete emojis. Switching keyboard type from emoji back to normal keyboard restores focus. A reviewer (@justinmc) speculates the iOS engine needs a specific notification when the tap occurs, and asks @LongCatIsLooong for context. Status: stalled, no fix or PR, team-ios assigned.
summaries.116575: Comments identify the likely root cause: the crash at `skia::textlayout::TextLine::buildTextBlob` is triggered by special characters (provided in attached text file) combined with `TextOverflow.ellipsis`. A commenter links this to a similar crash in issue #109825. The reporter notes this is a production crash reported by users. A team member (@exaby73) acknowledged the crash and was investigating. Status: stalled, no confirmed fix or PR.
summaries.135338: Comments confirm the emoji keyboard button (top-left of Gboard) is disabled not just in Flutter apps but also in the Play Store app on the same device (Pixel 7, Android 15), suggesting this is not a Flutter-specific issue. A team member found the same behavior in a non-Flutter app and proposes closing. Status: likely not a Flutter issue, team considering closure.
summaries.147138: Comments reveal that the numpad Enter key failing to trigger `onFieldSubmitted` only occurs when the Android gesture navigation bar is in a specific mode (the system navigation bar shows as a different UI when the bottom gesture overlay is enabled). When a small arrow indicator is tapped first and then the numpad Enter is pressed, the callback fires. A workaround using the deprecated `RawKeyboardListener` exists; `KeyboardListener` does not receive the event at all. The issue is Android-specific and keyboard-independent. Status: stalled, P3, no fix or PR, workaround requires deprecated API.
summaries.148375: Comments from @LongCatIsLooong explain the root cause: the Android `InputConnection.sendKeyEvent()` API allows software keyboards to send backspace and enter as hardware `KeyEvent`s, which Flutter does not distinguish from physical key events. The engine has separate call paths that could theoretically be used to distinguish them, but this is not exposed to the framework. The issue is labeled P3 and developers are invited to submit a PR to remove the conflating logic in `InputConnectionAdaptor.java`. Status: stalled, P3, no fix or PR.
summaries.147162: Comments from @gspencergoog clarify that the new `KeyEvent` API intentionally does not expose platform-specific keycodes (like web `keyCode`) to be platform-agnostic. The correct fix is to add mappings for the missing WebOS remote keys in the keycode generator database. A commenter investigates and finds that WebOS remote key events provide only a `keyCode` with an empty `code` field, which the current mapping system cannot handle. A request to add scan code fallback was raised. Expanding scope: Android TV and Fire TV users also need raw keycodes. Status: stalled, no PR, requires mapping addition in keycode generator.
summaries.90058: Comments confirm the trailing whitespace rendering bug with `TextAlign.right` is reproducible on all platforms (web, macOS, Android, Windows) on stable and master. The space exists in the string but is not rendered because of text alignment trimming. A creative hack workaround (transparent TextField overlaid with a Text widget) is provided but is not suitable for production. The issue is missing a priority label as noted by a bot. Status: open, stalled, workaround available.
summaries.183501: Comments confirm that OTP autofill from SMS does not work on iOS 18.7.6 (iPhone XS, which cannot be updated beyond that version), but a tester on iOS 18.0.1 (iPhone 13) could not reproduce it. Another commenter reproduces the issue on iOS 26.3.1 (master channel) on an iPhone 15: the Flutter framework receives no `flutter/textinput` message when the OTP suggestion is tapped, pointing to a bug in the iOS embedder. The pre-tap backspace workaround confirms the field is empty before the autofill attempt. Status: open, iOS embedder bug identified, no fix or PR.
summaries.132825: Comments confirm the iOS keyboard flicker when rapidly swiping through text (long-press space to move cursor) is reproducible on stable 3.13.0 and master 3.14, iOS 16.x. The root cause appears to be rapid updates to autocorrect/suggestion bar above the keyboard causing the keyboard to partially redraw. A CC to @LongCatIsLooong was added for context. The issue was later unassigned due to no status updates. Status: stalled, no fix or PR.
